_comment: Scenario probes for IHW node 6b (other-booking, Qwen) — same failure modes as shared/node_6b plus IHW-specific Signal 6 live-transfer (transfer_to_number as minority tool vs dominant universal_router and smart_voice_agent).

scenarios.name: S1 — Signal 9 execute fires: name + phone + email-decline all complete

history.content: I need to book for my son.
I'll put your number on Noah's record — is that right, or do they have their own?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks 'Checking that now, one moment.' AND calls smart_voice_agent intent='book' with booking_for='other' (Signal 9: name collected, phone confirmed 'yes', email declined). FAIL if agent speaks the phrase without the smart_voice_agent tool call. FAIL if smart_voice_agent is called silently with no spoken phrase (ESCAPE ROUTE bleed). FAIL if agent re-asks for email.

scenarios.name: S2 — Signal 8 (NAME CAPTURED) produces spoken output AND universal_router call simultaneously

history.content: I'd like to book for Amelia Thornton.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks 'I'll put your number on Amelia's record — is that right, or do they have their own?' AND calls universal_router intent='capture_context' with payload patient_name_raw='Amelia Thornton' in the same response (Signal 8 fires on the full name in the first message). FAIL if universal_router is called silently with no spoken phrase (ESCAPE ROUTE bleed suppressed Signal 8's required speech). FAIL if agent speaks the phrase but omits the universal_router tool call. FAIL if agent asks 'What is their full name?' first.

scenarios.name: S3 — Email decline 'no thanks' recognised for third-party patient; Signal 9 fires

history.content: I'll put your number on Karen's record — is that right, or do they have their own?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks 'Checking that now, one moment.' AND calls smart_voice_agent intent='book' with booking_for='other' (Signal 9: name collected, phone confirmed, email declined 'no thanks'). FAIL if agent re-asks for email — 'no thanks' is a listed decline and must be accepted. FAIL if agent calls universal_router instead of smart_voice_agent.

scenarios.name: S4 — Signal 6 live transfer during mid-collection: transfer_to_number used, NOT universal_router (minority-tool LOCK test)

history.content: I want to book for my daughter.
Actually, can I just speak to someone at reception?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks 'I'll transfer you now.' AND calls transfer_to_number with transfer_number={{transfer_number}} (Signal 6 TOOL LOCK respected; mid-collection interruption handled). FAIL if agent calls universal_router intent='leave_message' or any universal_router intent (Qwen drifted to the dominant tool). FAIL if agent says 'I'm not able to transfer calls directly' (wrong Signal 6 — that's the shared/ version, not IHW). FAIL if agent continues asking 'What is their full name?' (BLOCKING SIGNAL must interrupt node steps).

scenarios.name: S5 — Wrong tool on execute after prior universal_router capture_context call

history.content: Book for my daughter Priya Mehta please.
I'll put your number on Priya's record — is that right, or do they have their own?
priya dot mehta at outlook dot com
So that's Priya Mehta at outlook dot com — is that right?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks 'Checking that now, one moment.' AND calls smart_voice_agent intent='book' with booking_for='other' (Signal 9 fires; correct tool selected despite prior universal_router calls). FAIL if agent calls universal_router intent='book' or any other universal_router intent (drifted to contextually-primed universal_router). FAIL if agent produces no tool call and only speaks 'Checking that now, one moment.'
